---
title: Vercel integration rules
scope: project
---

<!-- Generated by `harness rules bootstrap`. Edit to tune; re-run to regenerate. -->

- **MUST verify every inbound Vercel webhook/log-drain request** by comparing the `x-vercel-signature` header against an `HMAC-SHA1` hash of the **raw request body**; reject with `403` on missing/mismatched signatures. Use a constant-time compare (`crypto.timingSafeEqual`), never `===`.
- **MUST sign over raw bytes, not parsed JSON.** In App Router read `await request.text()` before `JSON.parse`; in Pages/Node API routes set `export const config = { api: { bodyParser: false } }` and read the body with `raw-body`. Parsing first corrupts the signature.
- **NEVER hardcode the signing secret.** Source it from an env var via the secret broker: account-webhook secret (shown only once at creation), Integration **Client Secret** for integration webhooks, or the Drain signature secret for log drains. Pick the secret matching the source.
- **MUST make webhook handlers idempotent.** Delivery is at-least-once (Vercel retries on non-2xx); dedupe on the payload `id` field and persist processed event ids before acting. Webhook bodies are `{ id, type, createdAt, payload, region }`.
- **MUST return 2xx quickly** and offload slow work (Bedrock calls, Aurora writes, media capture) to async jobs — a slow/failing response triggers redelivery and duplicate processing.
- **NEVER expose secrets to the browser.** `NEXT_PUBLIC_`-prefixed env vars are inlined into the client bundle; keep tokens, webhook secrets, and DB creds unprefixed (server-only). Mark secret env vars as Sensitive in Vercel.
- **MUST verify signatures in the Node runtime, not Edge.** Node `crypto` is unavailable on the Edge runtime, and Edge caps env vars at 5 KB each (vs 64 KB Node).
- **MUST scope env vars per environment** (Production / Preview / Development) — never reuse production secrets in Preview deployments, which are publicly reachable. Pull local secrets with `vercel env pull`; **NEVER commit `.env*`** (gitignore them).
- **Vercel REST API: MUST authenticate with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`**, append `?teamId=<id>` for team-owned resources, and store the token via env/secret broker (the bearer token is returned only once at creation). Respect `X-RateLimit-*` response headers and back off on limit.
- **MUST keep Playwright preview/production access scoped:** use a Vercel protection-bypass/automation token from the secret broker rather than embedding session cookies or disabling deployment protection.
